Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

James Simmons has a patch to replace the ldlm has with rhashtable, but it was never finished and passed testing. Would it be better to use the new hashtable implementation instead of foxing the old code?  I'm not against this minor patch, but it would be nice if that code moved forward.

Patch Set 3:

> Would it be better to use the new hashtable implementation instead of foxing the old code?  
indeed rhashtable would be better, but it's going to take some time while I'm hitting this assertion few times a day :)

parents.subject: LU-16796 ofd: Change struct ofd_seq to use refcount_t

commit.subject: LU-17203 libcfs: ignore remaining items
commit.message:
LU-17203 libcfs: ignore remaining items

remove the assertion checking libcfs hashtable for emptiness
in cfs_hash_for_each_empty(). the only user of this hashtable
is per-export ldlm locks set. in this case it's legal that
some locks can't be removed from the hashtable being in the
process of enqueuing. the hashtable is destroyed from the
export destroy function which in turn is called only when all
RPCs on this export are done (exp_rpc_count==0).

Fixes: 306a9b666e ("LU-16272 libcfs: cfs_hash_for_each_empty optimization")
Signed-off-by: Alex Zhuravlev <bzzz@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I2b853b017bb7247a0c60cc8f464c2e08d649f0eb
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/52726
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Arshad Hussain <arshad.hussain@aeoncomputing.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
